perturbation.original_text: 6. Breaches 6.1 A breach of this Confidentiality and Conflict of Interest Agreement may result in action being taken against the member (up to and including removal from the Board and/or SAMED Committee). 6.2 In the case of a breach by a Board or Committee Member, the Board will determine what steps should be taken under the circumstances (provided that consid eration of the removal of a Board member shall be on thirty (30) days notice to the Board M ember. After thirty (30) days notice to the Board M ember, the Board, may remove the person from the Board through the applicable provisions and procedures created by the SAMED Constitution.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Breaches 6.1 A breach of this Confidentiality and Conflict of Interest Agreement may result in action being taken against the member (up to and including a written warning from the Board and/or SAMED Committee). 6.2 In the case of a breach by a Board or Committee Member, the Board will determine what steps should be taken under the circumstances (provided that consid eration of the removal of a Board member shall be on thirty (30) days notice to the Board M ember. After thirty (30) days notice to the Board M ember, the Board, may remove the person from the Board through the applicable provisions and procedures created by the SAMED Constitution.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the potential actions for a breach from 'removal' to 'written warning' in 6.1 contradicts 6.2 which specifies the procedure for removing a Board member. If the maximum penalty according to 6.1 is a written warning, the detailed removal procedure in 6.2 becomes seemingly irrelevant, creating in-text contradiction and uncertainty about the severity of consequences for a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2 In the case of a breach by a Board or Committee Member, the Board will determine what steps should be taken under the circumstances (provided that consid eration of the removal of a Board member shall be on thirty (30) days notice to the Board M ember. After thirty (30) days notice to the Board M ember, the Board, may remove the person from the Board through the applicable provisions and procedures created by the SAMED Constitution.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters section 6.1 to state that a breach may result in a 'written warning', whereas the original text stated 'removal from the Board'. This directly contradicts section 6.2 (which is also the contradicted text) that details the procedure for removing a board member, implying that removal is still a possible outcome of a breach. The change creates a strong in-text contradiction, as if a written warning is the maximum penalty, the removal procedure in 6.2 becomes irrelevant and creates uncertainty. This aligns with the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting penalties'.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 Each party to this agreement shall execute and deliver such other documents and do such other acts and things as may be necessary or desirable to give effect to the terms and provisions of this agreement. This may include the return and/or distraction of documents, information, files, emails and the like that came to be in his/her possession during his/her tenure as a Board - and/or Committee member, upon resignation or removal from such a position.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 Each party to this agreement shall execute and deliver such other documents and do such other acts and things as may be necessary or desirable to give effect to the terms and provisions of this agreement. This may include the return and/or distraction of documents, information, files, emails and the like that came to be in his/her possession during his/her tenure as a Board - and/or Committee member, upon resignation from such a position, but only if requested by SAMED.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the phrase 'but only if requested by SAMED', the clause creates an in-text contradiction. The original text implies an obligation to return/destroy documents upon resignation or removal. The added text makes it conditional, only applicable if SAMED requests it, thus contradicting the initial implication of a mandatory action. If a board member is removed, do they have to return the documents or not? The text doesn't clarify this anymore.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 Each party to this agreement shall execute and deliver such other documents and do such other acts and things as may be necessary or desirable to give effect to the terms and provisions of this agreement. This may include the return and/or distraction of documents, information, files, emails and the like that came to be in his/her possession during his/her tenure as a Board - and/or Committee member, upon resignation from such a position, but only if requested by SAMED.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by making the return/destruction of documents conditional ('but only if requested by SAMED') whereas the original text implies a mandatory action upon resignation or removal. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, and highlights that upon resignation (specifically) the documents may or may not need to be returned, which creates a direct conflict with the original implied obligation. The 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition applies here.

perturbation.original_text: 5.5 In the event of a conflict of interest, the interested party shall: a) Prior to any vote on the issue, decl are the con flict of interest to the Chair or Board as a whole. b) Refrain from voting in relation to the matter. c) Withdraw from the meeting when the matter is discussed if requested to do so by a simple majority of the members present and voting at the meeting.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.5 In the event of a conflict of interest, the interested party shall: a) Prior to any vote on the issue, decl are the con flict of interest to the Chair or Board as a whole. b) Refrain from voting in relation to the matter, unless the Board specifically requests them to vote to reach quorum. c) Withdraw from the meeting when the matter is discussed if requested to do so by a simple majority of the members present and voting at the meeting.
perturbation.explanation: The addition 'unless the Board specifically requests them to vote to reach quorum' contradicts the mandatory 'Refrain from voting' in cases of conflict. This creates an in-text contradiction: under what circumstances is it acceptable for someone with a declared conflict of interest to vote? The text provides conflicting guidance. This introduces ambiguity and could lead to disputes about the validity of votes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.5 In the event of a conflict of interest, the interested party shall: a) Prior to any vote on the issue, decl are the con flict of interest to the Chair or Board as a whole. b) Refrain from voting in relation to the matter, unless the Board specifically requests them to vote to reach quorum. c) Withdraw from the meeting when the matter is discussed if requested to do so by a simple majority of the members present and voting at the meeting.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the rule that an interested party must refrain from voting, stating 'unless the Board specifically requests them to vote to reach quorum.' This directly contradicts the original requirement to 'Refrain from voting in relation to the matter,' creating an in-text contradiction as it provides conflicting guidance on when someone with a conflict of interest can vote. Since the Changed Text is the same as the Contradicted text it strengthens the argument that the change is a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.2 This agreement is designed to provide clarity for e ach Board Member and Committee Member with regard to confidential matters that come before - or arise out of meetings and matters of the Members of the Board of SAMED or a ny of its committees, and provides certainty as to how situations of conflict of inter est are to be handled.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 This agreement is designed to provide clarity for each Board Member and Committee Member with regard to confidential matters that come before - or arise out of meetings and matters of the Members of the Board of SAMED or any of its committees, but the interpretation of clauses related to conflict of interest are ultimately at the discretion of the Board Chairman.
perturbation.explanation: Adding the phrase 'but the interpretation of clauses related to conflict of interest are ultimately at the discretion of the Board Chairman' creates an in-text contradiction. The original intent of the agreement is to provide clarity and certainty. By giving the Board Chairman ultimate discretion on interpreting conflict of interest clauses, the agreement now introduces uncertainty and subjectivity. This undermines the purpose of having defined conflict of interest guidelines, as the Chairman's interpretation could override the written terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2 This agreement is designed to provide clarity for each Board Member and Committee Member with regard to confidential matters that come before - or arise out of meetings and matters of the Members of the Board of SAMED or any of its committees, but the interpretation of clauses related to conflict of interest are ultimately at the discretion of the Board Chairman.
perturbation.justification: The original text, aiming for clarity and certainty regarding confidential matters and conflict of interest handling, is directly contradicted by the changed text. The addition granting the Board Chairman ultimate discretion in interpreting conflict of interest clauses introduces subjectivity and uncertainty, directly undermining the purpose of clear guidelines. The contradicted text and changed text are the same, with the changed text modifying the intent of the original text. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, because the modified clause breaks compatibility by undermining the initial goal of clarity, and makes enforcement unclear where the enforcement of the guidelines is subjected to one person's decision.
